# Derivation 3.7A — Nonstationary linear baseline and benchmarks {#sec-derivation-03-07A-nonstationary-linear-baseline .unnumbered}

## Purpose

This appendix supports Chapter 3.7. It records the locked linear nonstationary scalar-and-shift field block, the retarded solution forms, the stationary recovery check, and the benchmark verdict that makes the package reusable.

## Role / regime / status

- **Role:** nonstationary baseline reference.
- **Regime:** weak-to-moderate field; scalar+shift first; flat-background first pass; true-frame-facing time variable `t_*`.
- **Status:** locked and benchmarked as a solid first-pass closure package.
- **Non-claim:** this is not full nonlinear, tensor, radiative, or exact rotating-source completion.

## Locked variables and conventions

The linear baseline uses

$$
\theta_0(x,t_*),
\qquad
A_i(x,t_*).
$$

The scalar channel is the well/depth channel. The shift channel is the transverse odd-current channel. The baseline locks are

$$
c_M=c_P=c,
\qquad
\partial_iA_i=0.
$$

The field-layer source inputs are operationally compressed as

$$
\rho_M,
\qquad
j_i^\perp.
$$

Primitive `\mathcal{J}_k^\pm`, `\mathcal{M}_k`, and `\mathcal{P}_k` detail remains upstream in the source grammar.

## Exact linear field block

The locked scalar equation is

$$
\frac{1}{c^2}\partial_{t_*}^2\theta_0-\Delta\theta_0
=
\frac{4\pi G_N}{c^2}\rho_M.
$$

The locked shift equation is

$$
\frac{1}{c^2}\partial_{t_*}^2A_i-\Delta A_i
=
-\frac{16\pi G_N}{c^3}j_i^\perp,
\qquad
\partial_iA_i=0.
$$

No nonlinear terms, tensor backreaction terms, alternate speeds, or gauge variants belong in this baseline block.

## Retarded solutions

The scalar retarded solution is

$$
\theta_0(x,t_*)
=
-\frac{G_N}{c^2}
\int \frac{\rho_M(x',t_*-|x-x'|/c)}{|x-x'|}\,d^3x'.
$$

The shift retarded solution is

$$
A_i(x,t_*)
=
\frac{4G_N}{c^3}
\int \frac{j_i^\perp(x',t_*-|x-x'|/c)}{|x-x'|}\,d^3x'.
$$

Thus both scalar and shift disturbances propagate retardedly at speed `c` in the baseline reference packet.

## Stationary recovery

When the time derivatives vanish, the block reduces to

$$
\Delta\theta_0=-\frac{4\pi G_N}{c^2}\rho_M,
$$

and

$$
\Delta A_i=\frac{16\pi G_N}{c^3}j_i^\perp,
\qquad
\partial_iA_i=0.
$$

In the stationary overlap regime, this recovers the frozen scalar-well and shift packet. Stationary gravity is therefore not replaced by the nonstationary packet; it is recovered as its settled limit.

## Field-work identities

The scalar field-work density and flux may be written

$$
u_M=\frac{1}{2c^2}(\partial_{t_*}\theta_0)^2+\frac12|\nabla\theta_0|^2,
\qquad
S_M^i=-(\partial_{t_*}\theta_0)\partial_i\theta_0.
$$

They satisfy

$$
\partial_{t_*}u_M+\partial_iS_M^i
=
\frac{4\pi G_N}{c^2}\rho_M\partial_{t_*}\theta_0.
$$

For the shift channel,

$$
u_P=\frac{1}{2c^2}(\partial_{t_*}A_i)^2+\frac12(\partial_kA_i)(\partial_kA_i),
$$

with a corresponding flux identity

$$
\partial_{t_*}u_P+\partial_kS_P^k
=
-\frac{16\pi G_N}{c^3}j_i^\perp\partial_{t_*}A_i.
$$

These identities are why the baseline is more than a formal wave equation: it has usable field-side bookkeeping.

## Benchmark verdict

The baseline passes the current first-pass benchmark set:

1. scalar retarded pulse,
2. transverse shift/current retarded pulse,
3. exact stationary recovery,
4. local and integrated field-work consistency,
5. bounded far-zone rotating-disturbance asymptotics.

The fifth check passes only at bounded asymptotic/scaling level. It does not prove exact native Kerr closure or full tensor/radiative completion.

## Result

The linear nonstationary scalar+shift package is stable enough to serve as the canonical nonstationary starting point. Its correct label is **solid first-pass closure**, not final gravity completion.
